preamble: The near-open front unrounded vowel [æ] raises and backs to the close-mid back rounded vowel [o] through a combined process of vowel raising, backness shift, and rounding addition. This complex vowel change is documented in historical sound changes and contemporary sociophonetic variation.

examples.note: Extreme vowel shift in certain non-standard English dialects and rapid speech, affecting words with /æ/ in stressed syllables.
Regional variation in certain Dutch dialects where front vowels shift to back rounded vowels.
Sociolinguistic variation in certain German regions affecting vowel quality.
Historical vowel shift in certain Scandinavian dialects.
